critical_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Oracle WebLogic Server Deserialization Vulnerability (CVE-2020-2551)
critical_severity_vulnerabilities.description: An Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability (CVE-2020-2551) exists in the WLS Core Components, which can allow an unauthenticated attacker with network access via T3 to compromise Oracle WebLogic Server.
critical_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest security patches from Oracle for WebLogic Server to address CVE-2020-2551. Restrict T3 protocol access to trusted hosts only.

high_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Oracle WebLogic Server Information Disclosure (CVE-2022-21371)
high_severity_vulnerabilities.description: An Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability (CVE-2022-21371) exists in the Web Container component, allowing an unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTP to disclose sensitive information.
high_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest security patches from Oracle for WebLogic Server to address CVE-2022-21371. Ensure sensitive configuration files like WEB-INF/weblogic.xml are not publicly accessible.

high_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Oracle WebLogic Server Remote File Inclusion (CVE-2023-22047)
high_severity_vulnerabilities.description: An Oracle WebLogic Server vulnerability (CVE-2023-22047) in the WSRP component can lead to remote file inclusion via an unauthenticated HTTP request.
high_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest security patches from Oracle for WebLogic Server to address CVE-2023-22047. Ensure proper input validation and restrict access to the WSRP component.

high_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Apache byterange filter DoS
high_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The Apache web server is vulnerable to a denial of service attack when numerous overlapping byte ranges are requested.
high_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade Apache HTTP Server to a version that fixes CVE-2011-3192 or apply the recommended mitigation (e.g., mod_reqtimeout).
high_severity_vulnerabilities.raw:
http-vuln-cve2011-3192: 
  VULNERABLE:
  Apache byterange filter DoS
    State: VULNERABLE
    IDs:  BID:49303  CVE:CVE-2011-3192
      The Apache web server is vulnerable to a denial of service attack when numerous
      overlapping byte ranges are requested.
    Disclosure date: 2011-08-19
    References:
      https://www.tenable.com/plugins/nessus/55976
      https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2011-3192
      https://seclists.org/fulldisclosure/2011/Aug/175
      https://www.securityfocus.com/bid/49303

medium_severity_vulnerabilities.title: SSL POODLE information leak
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The SSL protocol 3.0, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1i and other products, uses nondeterministic CBC padding, which makes it easier for man-in-the-middle attackers to obtain cleartext data via a padding-oracle attack, aka the "POODLE" issue.
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable SSL 3.0 support on affected systems and prioritize TLS 1.2 or higher. Ensure all clients also support newer TLS versions.
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.raw:
ssl-poodle: 
  VULNERABLE:
  SSL POODLE information leak
    State: LIKELY VULNERABLE
    IDs:  BID:70574  CVE:CVE-2014-3566
          The SSL protocol 3.0, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1i and other
          products, uses nondeterministic CBC padding, which makes it easier
          for man-in-the-middle attackers to obtain cleartext data via a
          padding-oracle attack, aka the "POODLE" issue.
    Disclosure date: 2014-10-14
    Check results:
      TLS_DHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
      TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV properly implemented
    References:
      https://www.imperialviolet.org/2014/10/14/poodle.html
      https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2014-3566
      https://www.openssl.org/~bodo/ssl-poodle.pdf
      https://www.securityfocus.com/bid/70574

medium_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Exposure of Behat Configuration File
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.description: A publicly accessible Behat configuration file (behat.yml.dist) was discovered, which may contain sensitive information about the application's testing environment, paths, or credentials.
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the publicly exposed Behat configuration file (behat.yml.dist) or restrict access to it. Review its contents for any sensitive data that might have been disclosed.

low_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Firewall bypass through FTP helper
low_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The firewall is vulnerable to bypass through its FTP helper functionality (IPv4), potentially allowing unauthorized access or circumvention of network controls.
low_severity_vulnerabilities.evidence: Firewall vulnerable to bypass through ftp helper. (IPv4)
low_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Review firewall rules and configurations related to FTP helpers. Ensure that FTP connections are properly inspected and that bypasses are not possible. Consider disabling FTP helper if not critically needed.
low_severity_vulnerabilities.raw:
firewall-bypass: 
  Firewall vulnerable to bypass through ftp helper. (IPv4)

low_severity_vulnerabilities.title: JMX Console Authentication Not Required
low_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The JMX console is accessible without authentication, potentially allowing unauthorized users to manage and monitor Java applications, leading to information disclosure or remote code execution.
low_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Secure the JMX console with strong authentication and authorization mechanisms. Restrict network access to the JMX console to trusted administrative networks only.

immediate: Address Oracle WebLogic Server Critical vulnerability (CVE-2020-2551) by applying vendor patches and restricting T3 protocol access.

Patch Oracle WebLogic Server for high-severity information disclosure (CVE-2022-21371) and remote file inclusion (CVE-2023-22047) vulnerabilities. Ensure no sensitive configuration files are publicly exposed.
Update Apache HTTP Server to a version not vulnerable to the byterange filter DoS (CVE-2011-3192) or implement recommended mitigations.
short_term: Disable SSL 3.0 on affected hosts (sis.nmu.edu.eg) to mitigate the POODLE vulnerability (CVE-2014-3566) and enforce TLS 1.2 or higher.
Remove or secure the publicly accessible Behat configuration file (behat.yml.dist) from nlms.nmu.edu.eg to prevent information leakage.
Secure the JMX console on qb.nmu.edu.eg with strong authentication and restrict network access to trusted administrative networks.
Review and harden firewall rules, particularly concerning FTP helpers, on sis.nmu.edu.eg to prevent potential bypasses.
medium_term: Implement a regular vulnerability scanning and patching program for all public-facing assets.
Conduct a comprehensive review of all publicly accessible configuration files and development artifacts to prevent information disclosure.
Enhance network segmentation and access controls to limit lateral movement and reduce the attack surface.
Develop and implement a security awareness training program for developers to prevent common misconfigurations and vulnerabilities.
